recaps.text: Across the earlier books, three children discovered the Enchanted Wood and the great Faraway Tree, whose top reaches ever-changing magic lands through a hole in a cloud. They befriended the folk of the trunk - Moon-Face and his slippery-slip, the elf Silky, the half-deaf Saucepan Man, the Angry Pixie and Dame Washalot - and even won over a doubting cousin. Each land above the tree could be a treat or a trap, and had to be left before it moved on.

A new visitor - a rather conceited, know-it-all girl named Connie - comes to stay and scoffs at the idea of a magic tree. The children take her into the Enchanted Wood, where she meets the tree folk and gets a taste of their ways, from the Angry Pixie to Dame Washalot's water. Tea with Moon-Face begins to shake her disbelief, though her superior manner still needs softening.

Connie is gradually humbled as the tree's lands and folk refuse to be impressed by her airs. The children climb to several new countries at the top of the tree, and their adventures land them in the Land of Dame Slap, whose strict school metes out slaps to troublesome pupils. Escaping her clutches takes all their cleverness and the help of their friends from the tree.

After more journeys through the lands above the Faraway Tree, Connie has learned to be far less boastful and to value her new friends. With their adventures at an end, the children say a fond good-bye to the Faraway Tree and its folk, closing the door on the Enchanted Wood.
